title: -Acceptance of Grant Funds and Contract: Healthy Black Families – Equity 4 Black Berkeley (E4BB) Transition

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Accepting $90,000 in grant funds from the San Francisco Foundation to support
the Equity 4 Black Berkeley (E4BB) initiative; and
2. Authorizing the City Manager, or designee, to execute a contract and any
amendments with Healthy Black Families (HBF) for an amount not to exceed
$90,000 to provide technical assistance, fiscal sponsorship, and organizational
development services to support the establishment of an independent nonprofit
organization to carry forward the work of Equity 4 Black Berkeley.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The $90,000 grant from the San Francisco Foundation will be deposited into a special
revenue fund established for this purpose and appropriated to the City Manager’s Office
for expenditure. No General Fund resources are required.
sections.current_situation:
The City of Berkeley partnered with the San Francisco Foundation (SFF) and Healthy
Black Families (HBF) to advance Equity 4 Black Berkeley (E4BB) — a community-
driven initiative addressing the historic and ongoing impacts of segregation, redlining,
and displacement of Black residents.
Healthy Black Families (HBF) was identified as the lead community partner based on its
established nonprofit governance structure, administrative capacity to manage grants,
and its direct role in convening the E4BB community leadership group. HBF has also
been integral to sustaining E4BB’s programming and partnerships during its transition
from City incubation to independent governance. Contracting with HBF ensures
continuity, fiscal accountability, and the community’s active leadership in the initiative’s
next phase.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Contract: Healthy Black Families – Equity 4 Black Berkeley
CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
E4BB builds on prior work to implement Berkeley’s Right to Return and Affordable
Housing Preference Policy, expanding the City’s commitment to equitable housing
access, community stability, and generational wealth repair.
To ensure sustainability beyond City incubation, SFF has awarded a $90,000 grant to
support the transition of E4BB into an independent, community-governed nonprofit
entity. Under this agreement, the City will serve as fiscal intermediary, contracting
directly with HBF to provide organizational development, fiscal sponsorship, and
technical assistance necessary for establishing the independent nonprofit. HBF may
engage specialized consultants to provide technical assistance – such as legal
incorporation support, organizational design, and capacity-building – but will remain the
primary implementing entity responsible for contract performance, deliverables, and
reporting. While HBF will
sections.background:
The Equity 4 Black Berkeley initiative originated in the Mayor’s Office and transitioned to
the City Manager’s Office to align with the City’s broader equity infrastructure.
Since its inception, E4BB has supported community engagement around the City’s
Right to Return and Affordable Housing Preference policy, convened a community
leadership group representing displaced Black residents, and partnered with the San
Francisco Foundation to define the governance and capacity-building roadmap now
moving into implementation. These foundational efforts established trust, visibility, and
the structure that the current grant will build upon.
Healthy Black Families—a 501(c)(3) nonprofit with deep community roots—has served
as the project anchor and fiscal sponsor for E4BB during its development phase. The
E4BB leadership group will be re-engaged during the next phase to help guide
implementation planning and ensure accountability to community-defined goals. This
next stage, funded through SFF’s $90,000 grant, focuses on capacity building,
governance, and the creation of a legally incorporated nonprofit capable of stewarding
E4BB’s mission and programs independently. Planning and alignment with City equity
frameworks ensures that the emerging nonprofit is structured to complement, rather
than duplicate, existing City equity initiatives and accountability systems. This step
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Contract: Healthy Black Families – Equity 4 Black Berkeley
CONSENT CALENDAR
December 2, 2025
helps establish a durable foundation for independent governance that remains
strategically connected to City priorities.
The project’s scope includes:
 Phase 1: Planning and alignment with City equity frameworks.
 Phase 2: Legal formation and governance structure.
 Phase 3: Administrative systems and capacity building.
 Phase 4: Transition and independent launch.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental impacts or opportunities associated with this
sections.rationale:
Accepting the grant and contracting with HBF will ensure that Equity 4 Black Berkeley
transitions from City incubation to a sustainable, community-led structure. This
approach leverages HBF’s community trust, administrative capacity, and established
governance systems to manage the project effectively while the new E4BB entity is
developed.
This action also advances the City’s equity commitments in a fiscally and operationally
responsible manner. Accepting these funds now solidifies the City’s leadership in
supporting Black-led community governance.
sections.alternatives:
Not accepting the grant or delaying execution would stall momentum and risk loss of
external funding support from SFF.
CONTACT PERSON
Rex Brown, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Officer – City Manager’s Office
(510) 981-7019
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ACCEPTANCE OF GRANT FUNDS AND CONTRACT: HEALTHY BLACK FAMILIES –
EQUITY 4 BLACK BERKELEY TRANSITION
WHEREAS, the San Francisco Foundation (SFF) has awarded a $90,000 grant to the
City of Berkeley to be administered through a contract with Healthy Black Families (HBF),
the designated community partner for the Equity 4 Black Berkeley (E4BB) initiative; and
WHEREAS, the E4BB initiative advances Berkeley’s Right to Return and Affordable
Housing Preference Policy, addressing historic racial inequities in housing, displacement,
and economic opportunity; and
WHEREAS, Healthy Black Families (HBF), a local 501(c)(3) nonprofit, has been
designated as the fiscal sponsor and contractor responsible for carrying out the work plan,
including capacity building, governance development, and organizational formation; and
WHEREAS, the City Manager’s Office will oversee the contract and ensure compliance
with grant requirements in coordination with SFF; and
WHEREAS, the contract will include monthly progress reports and quarterly coordination
meetings to ensure transparency and accountability; and
WHEREAS, acceptance of these grant funds will have no impact on the City’s General
Fund.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City hereby accepts $90,000 in grant funds from the San Francisco Foundation and
authorizes the City Manager or his designee to execute a contract and any amendments
with Healthy Black Families, in an amount not to exceed $90,000, to implement the Equity
4 Black Berkeley Transition Project, through June 30, 2026.
